content.claim: Hungary's PISA performance profile shows a lower share of top performers (9.3% at or above Level 5 versus OECD average 12.6%) and a higher share of low performers (28.1% below Level 2 versus OECD average 23.0%), indicating that early selection has not improved the distribution of high performance.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: PISA cycle year not specified in research notes
Low and top performance rates could reflect measurement timing within early-tracking cohorts rather than tracking effect alone
No comparison of performance trajectory before and after establishment of 6/8-year gimnázium tracks
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
